mission.title: Install and Configure Keylime on Kubernetes
mission.description: Keylime is an open-source scalable trust system that leverages TPM technology to bootstrap and maintain trust on edge, cloud, and IoT devices. Installing Keylime allows you to ensure the integrity of remote machines and securely manage cryptographic trust.

steps.title: Create a Namespace for Keylime
steps.description: First, create a dedicated namespace for Keylime to keep its resources organized.

steps.title: Apply Keylime Manifests
steps.description: Next, apply the Keylime manifests from the GitHub repository. Ensure you are using a specific version.

steps.title: Verify Keylime Installation
steps.description: Check that all Keylime pods are running correctly in the keylime namespace.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Keylime will show all Keylime pods running in the 'keylime' namespace. You will also have configured resource limits and RBAC for secure operation.

uninstall.title: Remove Keylime Resources
uninstall.description: To uninstall Keylime, delete the resources applied in the keylime namespace.

uninstall.title: Delete the Keylime Namespace
uninstall.description: Finally, delete the namespace to clean up all resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Keylime
upgrade.description: To upgrade Keylime, apply the new version of the manifests. Ensure to specify the new version in the URLs.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description: If a pod is not starting, check the pod logs for errors using the command: `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n keylime`.

troubleshooting.title: Resource Quotas
troubleshooting.description: Ensure that your cluster has enough resources (CPU, memory) to run Keylime pods. Check resource usage with `kubectl top pods -n keylime`.

troubleshooting.title: Network Issues
troubleshooting.description: If the Keylime components cannot communicate, check your network policies and ensure that the necessary ports are open.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your cluster.
